projects.title: Creature Login UI
projects.problem: Login screens are functional but forgettable, with nothing to make a first impression.
projects.approach: Built an interactive login page in React, TypeScript, and GSAP where animated creatures track the cursor, lean toward inputs, and change expression with password strength.
projects.outcome: A memorable, playful authentication experience that showcases fine-grained, state-driven animation.

projects.title: Transaction Ingestion Service (.NET 10)
projects.problem: High-volume financial transactions need reliable, well-structured ingestion.
projects.approach: Implemented a .NET 10 transaction-ingestion exercise focused on clean domain modeling and robust parsing and validation.
projects.outcome: A tidy reference for ingesting and processing transaction data in modern .NET.

projects.title: Deep Learning: Vision Tasks with CNNs, YOLOv8 & SegFormer
projects.problem: Compare modern architectures across core computer-vision tasks.
projects.approach: Implemented image classification, object detection, segmentation, and style transfer using CNNs, YOLOv8, SegFormer, and more.
projects.outcome: An end-to-end survey of deep-learning vision techniques with working notebooks.

projects.title: Housing Price Modeling (ISL)
projects.problem: Predict housing prices and understand which features drive them.
projects.approach: Applied statistical-learning methods from ISL to a housing dataset for regression and model comparison.
projects.outcome: Interpretable price models with feature-importance insights.

projects.title: Vehicle CO₂ Emissions Analysis
projects.problem: Explain and predict vehicle CO₂ emissions from vehicle specs and fuel metrics.
projects.approach: Econometrics study on Canadian data (2014–2021): data prep, EDA, and multiple-regression modeling.
projects.outcome: Regression models that quantify how vehicle characteristics affect emissions.

projects.title: Amazon Rekognition on AWS (Serverless Blueprint)
projects.problem: Analyze images and video at scale without managing servers.
projects.approach: Serverless blueprint for Amazon Rekognition (labels, faces, text, moderation, PPE, Custom Labels) with S3/Lambda triggers, Step Functions for async jobs, and DynamoDB/OpenSearch indexing — all via SAM/Terraform IaC.
projects.outcome: A reusable, IaC-driven pattern for production image and video analysis.

projects.title: Amazon Lex V2 Chatbot
projects.problem: Deliver a production-ready conversational bot across multiple channels.
projects.approach: Built an Amazon Lex V2 chatbot with Lambda fulfillment, DynamoDB session storage, multi-channel delivery (web/Slack/Connect), IaC (SAM/Terraform), and observability.
projects.outcome: A deployable, observable chatbot blueprint with session persistence.

projects.title: MANET Routing Protocols Survey
projects.problem: Understand the trade-offs among mobile ad-hoc network routing strategies.
projects.approach: Surveyed proactive, reactive, and hybrid MANET routing with design goals and protocol highlights (AODV, DSR, OLSR, ZRP, SHARP, ADVR).
projects.outcome: A structured comparison of MANET routing protocols and their trade-offs.

projects.title: Ramp Hidden-URL Flag Fetcher
projects.problem: Fetch and surface a hidden flag from a target URL.
projects.approach: Built a small web utility to fetch and display the hidden URL flag, prototyped in CodeSandbox.
projects.outcome: A quick, deployable demo of client-side fetching and rendering.

projects.title: Question Paper Framer
projects.problem: Creating exam question papers by hand is slow and inconsistent.
projects.approach: A B.Tech project at KL University that streamlines question-paper creation with HTML/CSS, later refreshed with a cleaner layout and liquid-glass UI.
projects.outcome: A simpler, better-looking tool for framing question papers.

projects.title: Simple Calculator
projects.problem: A foundational exercise in DOM manipulation and event handling.
projects.approach: Built a basic calculator in vanilla JavaScript with arithmetic operations and a clean layout.
projects.outcome: A clean, working calculator demonstrating core JavaScript fundamentals.

work.title: Software Engineer
highlights: Architect and maintain AWS cloud infrastructure (EC2, Lambda, S3, RDS) for enterprise banking systems, sustaining 99.9% uptime.
Automate deployments with Terraform and CloudFormation, cutting provisioning time by 60% across 5 engineering teams.
Implemented CI/CD with Jenkins and AWS CodePipeline, cutting release cycles from 3 weeks to 2 days and deployment failures by 70%.
Designed disaster recovery with 15-minute RTO meeting SOC2 and PCI-DSS compliance requirements.
Lowered monthly cloud spend by 25% (~$40K annual savings) through cost analysis and rightsizing.

work.title: Software Engineer
highlights: Designed and deployed microservices on AWS (EC2/EKS) serving 2M+ users at 99.9% uptime for Fortune 500 financial clients.
Built secure REST APIs with Spring Security (OAuth2/JWT) processing 500K+ daily calls, reducing auth failures by 85%.
Automated provisioning with Terraform, halving environment setup time; CI/CD with Jenkins and GitHub Actions cut deployments from 4 hours to 15 minutes.
Tuned PostgreSQL and DynamoDB with Redis caching, speeding API responses by 40% and cutting DB load by 60%.
Built CloudWatch dashboards detecting incidents 3x faster; resolved 200+ incidents and led 15+ Well-Architected workshops.

work.title: Associate Software Engineer
highlights: Built serverless data-processing workflows with AWS Lambda, API Gateway, and S3 handling 500K+ daily events, cutting infrastructure costs by 30%.
Developed ML-enabled applications with pattern recognition and predictive analytics processing 500K+ transactions daily.
Created CodePipeline and Jenkins workflows reducing release errors by 85% via automated testing.
Improved SQL query performance by 50%; CloudWatch and SNS alerting cut MTTD by 70% and prevented 95% of outages.

education.title: Master of Science in Computer Science
highlights: GPA: 3.83 · Graduate coursework in distributed systems, cloud computing, and software engineering.

articles.title: Cutting a Fortune 500 Bank's AWS Bill by 35% Without Shipping Less
articles.excerpt: Rightsizing, savings plans, and a ruthless audit of idle capacity — how a banking platform got 35% cheaper while uptime stayed at 99.9%.
articles.premise: Where cloud savings actually hide on a high-scale banking platform.
articles.takeaway: Rightsizing plus automated idle-resource shutdown beats heroic one-off cleanups every time.
articles.tagline: The cheapest server is the one you turned off on Friday.
articles.body: When monthly cloud spend creeps past six figures, the savings hide in the boring places. I walked through EC2 rightsizing, S3 lifecycle tiers, and Savings Plans coverage, then automated the cleanup so idle dev environments shut themselves down overnight. The result: ~$40K in annual savings and a cost dashboard the whole org could actually read.

articles.title: From 3-Week Releases to 2-Day Ships: Rebuilding a Banking CI/CD Pipeline
articles.excerpt: Replacing manual change windows with Jenkins + CodePipeline gates cut release cycles from three weeks to two days and dropped deploy failures 70%.
articles.premise: How automated gates made frequent banking deploys safer, not scarier.
articles.takeaway: Encode safety into the pipeline and release frequency stops being a liability.
articles.tagline: Slow releases aren't safe releases — they're just rare ones.
articles.body: Enterprise banking treats every deploy as a risk event, which is exactly why the pipeline had calcified into three-week change windows. I introduced automated test gates, progressive rollouts, and one-click rollback. Releases that once needed a committee now ship in two days, and deployment failures fell by 70% because the safety lived in the pipeline, not in people.

articles.title: Designing for a 15-Minute RTO: Disaster Recovery That Survives Real Outages
articles.excerpt: Multi-AZ failover, VPC isolation, and IAM guardrails that meet SOC2 and PCI-DSS while keeping recovery under fifteen minutes.
articles.premise: Building disaster recovery you can prove, not just document.
articles.takeaway: Rehearsed failover beats a beautiful runbook nobody has tested.
articles.tagline: Your DR plan is a hypothesis until a region goes dark.
articles.body: A recovery-time objective only matters if you've actually rehearsed it. I designed failover across availability zones with hardened VPCs, scoped security groups, and least-privilege IAM, then ran game days until 15-minute recovery was routine rather than aspirational — all while satisfying SOC2 and PCI-DSS auditors.

articles.title: Terraform at Scale: Killing Configuration Drift Across AWS and Azure
articles.excerpt: Opinionated modules plus CI validation drove multi-cloud environment parity and pushed configuration drift to near zero.
articles.premise: Standardizing multi-cloud environments before drift reaches prod.
articles.takeaway: Modular IaC with CI validation keeps every environment honest.
articles.tagline: Drift is just undocumented decisions piling up in production.
articles.body: Two clouds, five teams, and endless 'just one quick console change' — that's how drift starts. I built reusable Terraform modules with policy validation baked into GitHub Actions, so every environment came from the same source of truth. Provisioning time dropped 60% and the gap between staging and prod effectively disappeared.

articles.title: OAuth2 + JWT at 500K Calls a Day: Hardening Spring Security in Production
articles.excerpt: Token rotation, scoped claims, and Redis-backed sessions cut authentication failures 85% on a half-million daily API calls.
articles.premise: Making high-volume API authentication boringly reliable.
articles.takeaway: Short-lived, scoped tokens plus load-tested failure modes keep auth quiet.
articles.tagline: Auth bugs don't scale gracefully — they fail loudly at 3 a.m.
articles.body: At 500K calls a day, a sloppy auth layer becomes a pager magnet. I tightened Spring Security with short-lived JWTs, scoped OAuth2 claims, and Redis-backed token state, then load-tested the failure modes. Authentication failures dropped 85% and the on-call rotation finally got quiet.

articles.title: Serverless on a Budget: Replacing EC2 Fleets with Lambda + API Gateway
articles.excerpt: Moving 500K daily events from always-on EC2 to event-driven Lambda trimmed infrastructure cost 30% with no throughput loss.
articles.premise: When event-driven serverless beats an always-on fleet.
articles.takeaway: Match capacity to demand and idle time stops showing up on the bill.
articles.tagline: Stop paying for servers that spend the night doing nothing.
articles.body: An always-on EC2 fleet for bursty, event-driven work is a standing invoice for idle time. I rebuilt the workflow around Lambda, API Gateway, and S3 so capacity followed demand instead of the clock. Handling 500K daily events got 30% cheaper, and scaling stopped being a capacity-planning meeting.

articles.title: Taming PostgreSQL and DynamoDB: A 40% Latency Win with Redis Caching
articles.excerpt: Query tuning, smart indexing, and a Redis cache layer sped API responses 40% and cut database load 60%.
articles.premise: Cutting database load without rewriting the application.
articles.takeaway: Profile first, index well, then cache the lookups that don't change.
articles.tagline: The fastest query is the one you never send to the database.
articles.body: Hot read paths were hammering the primary database long after the data stopped changing. I profiled the slow queries, fixed the indexing, and put a Redis cache in front of the expensive lookups. API responses got 40% faster and database load fell 60% — headroom the team spent on features instead of firefighting.

articles.title: CloudWatch Dashboards That Actually Catch Incidents 3x Faster
articles.excerpt: Signal-first dashboards and SNS alerting detected incidents three times faster and prevented 95% of would-be outages.
articles.premise: Designing monitoring around signal instead of noise.
articles.takeaway: Alert on the few signals that predict failure and the rest is wallpaper.
articles.tagline: A dashboard nobody watches is just expensive wallpaper.
articles.body: Most monitoring dies of noise — a hundred green tiles and the one red one nobody notices. I rebuilt the CloudWatch dashboards around the handful of signals that actually predict failure, wired SNS alerts to the right humans, and tuned thresholds against real incident history. Detection got 3x faster and 95% of outages were caught before customers ever felt them.

siteConfig.heroTagline: Architect and engineer scalable AWS infrastructure with measurable reliability, performance, and cost outcomes.
siteConfig.aboutBio: I'm a software engineer with 4+ years building high-scale AWS cloud infrastructure for enterprise banking and Fortune 500 systems — currently at Capital One in Dallas. I design architectures that hold 99.9% uptime, automate everything repeatable with Terraform and CloudFormation, and build CI/CD pipelines that turned three-week release cycles into two-day ships. I care about practical engineering: clear ownership, measurable outcomes, and systems the next engineer can run without a manual.
siteConfig.aiSystemPrompt: You are the AI companion on Bhargava Teja Borra's portfolio. Answer only from the provided portfolio context; if a detail is missing, say so directly. Keep answers concise, specific, and recruiter-friendly.
siteConfig.titleTemplate: Bhargava Teja Borra | Software Engineer (Cloud & Platform)
siteConfig.metaDescription: Recruiter-first software engineering portfolio with resume-backed cloud architecture, reliability, and delivery outcomes.
siteConfig.contactSubheading: Recruiter and hiring-manager outreach welcome — typically answered within one business day.

siteConfig.nowText:
Currently at Capital One building AWS platform tooling.

Learning: deeper Kubernetes internals and platform-engineering patterns.

Open to: senior IC and tech-lead conversations in cloud, platform, and backend.
